title_header: Tower of God | Episode 4 | Headon's Floor

dialogue.speaker: BAAM
dialogue.line: IN THE DARKNESS, SHE WAS MY STAR. SHE TAUGHT ME HOW TO SPEAK, TOLD ME ABOUT THE OUTSIDE WORLD, TAUGHT ME THE WARMTH OF HUMANS, AND GAVE ME A REASON TO LIVE. SINCE MY BIRTH, I HAD BEEN TRAPPED IN DARKNESS. I HAD NEVER LAID DOWN UNDER THE NIGHT SKY BEFORE. BUT WHEN SHE JOINED MY DARKNESS, THE SKY ABOVE BECAME A BEAUTIFUL NIGHT SKY.

dialogue.speaker: BAAM
dialogue.line: YOU MIGHT DIE. I DON'T MIND.

dialogue.speaker: BAAM
dialogue.line: FOR ME, THERE'S SOMETHING WORSE THAN DEATH.

dialogue.speaker: HEADON
dialogue.line: ALTHOUGH I AGREED TO LET HIM GO, THERE SEEMS TO BE NO CHANCE HE'LL BEAT THIS TEST.

dialogue.speaker: EVAN
dialogue.line: ME, A 4-RANK ELITE. GOT ANY GOOD IDEAS ON HOW HE CAN WIN?

dialogue.speaker: HEADON
dialogue.line: HONESTLY, THE DIFFERENCE IN STRENGTH IS TOO DEEP. YOU CAN ONLY HOPE FOR A MIRACLE.

dialogue.speaker: HEADON
dialogue.line: ACTUALLY, THERE IS ONE WAY HE CAN WIN. THE ONLY WAY THAT THE BOY CAN PASS THIS TEST.

dialogue.speaker: HEADON
dialogue.line: THERE'S AN OVERWHELMING DIFFERENCE IN POWER BETWEEN THE TWO. OF COURSE, THE BOY WILL AVOID FIGHTING THE EEL. HE WILL TRY TO ESCAPE FROM IT AND GO DIRECTLY TO THE BALL TO POP IT. BUT THAT IS IMPOSSIBLE. IN THAT HIGH CONCENTRATION OF SHINSU, THE EEL IS EXTREMELY FAST. THE BOY CAN'T ESCAPE FROM IT INSIDE THE SHINSU. IF HE WANTS TO WIN, HE SHOULDN'T TRY TO ESCAPE. THE ONLY WAY TO PASS THIS TEST IS TO FIGHT THE EEL, INSTEAD. OF COURSE, HE CAN'T FIGHT THE EEL FACE-TO-FACE. HE COULD NEVER BEAT THE EEL THAT WAY. FOR THE HUNGRY EEL, THE BOY IS NOT AN "ENEMY," HE'S JUST A LITTLE SNACK. AND AS LONG AS HE DOESN'T RESIST, THE EEL WILL UNHESITATINGLY SWALLOW HIM WHOLE. AND THAT'S HIS CHANCE TO STRIKE. AFTER THE EEL DEFENSELESSLY SWALLOWS THE BOY, WITH POISE AND ACCURACY, THE BOY MUST STAB THE EEL'S TONGUE WITH THE BLACK MARCH. OTHER WEAPONS MIGHT NOT WORK, BUT WITH THE BLACK MARCH, THE BOY CAN DO CONSIDERABLE DAMAGE AND KNOCK OUT THE EEL FOR A WHILE. AND WHILE THE EEL IS IN PAIN, HE CAN RUN PAST THE EEL TO THE BALL AND POP IT. IF HE CAN DO THAT, HE CAN PASS THE TEST AND GO ON TO THE NEXT LEVEL.

dialogue.speaker: HEADON
dialogue.line: BUT I CAN'T REVEAL THIS PATH TO THE BOY. GUIDES CAN ONLY SHOW PATHS TO THOSE WHO CAN WALK THE PATH. BUT THIS BOY CANNOT WALK THE PATH, AND SO HE WILL EVENTUALLY FAIL.

dialogue.speaker: HEADON
dialogue.line: WELL, ACTUALLY, NO ONE CAN DO IT. ONLY THE INSANE COULD ACTUALLY DO SOMETHING THAT CRAZY. THAT'S WHY I DIDN'T TELL HIM. RATHER THAN LETTING HIM DIE WITH FALSE HOPE, IT IS BETTER FOR HIM TO BE SAVED BY LADY YURI AFTER RUNNING FOR HOURS AND REALIZING HIS LIMIT.

dialogue.speaker: HEADON
dialogue.line: NOW, RUN! DISCOVER YOUR LIMIT AND WEAKNESS, AND GIVE UP YOUR QUEST UP THE TOWER. THAT'S THE ONLY WAY YOU CAN SURVIVE.

dialogue.speaker: EVAN
dialogue.line: WHAT'S WRONG WITH HIM?! WHY IS HE JUST STANDING THERE? WHAT IS HE DOING?

dialogue.speaker: EVAN
dialogue.line: HEY! NEW BOY! WHAT ARE YOU DOING?!

dialogue.speaker: EVAN
dialogue.line: RUN! RUN AWAY AND GET TO THE BALL, YOU IDIOT!

dialogue.speaker: HEADON
dialogue.line: WHY ISN'T HE RUNNING AWAY? WHAT ARE YOU DOING? HAS HE FROZEN IN FEAR?

dialogue.speaker: HEADON
dialogue.line: HE'S TRYING TO GET INSIDE THE EEL!

dialogue.speaker: EVAN
dialogue.line: DID HE? DID HE KNOW THE PATH TO WIN? IS HE ABLE TO WALK THAT PATH?

dialogue.speaker: EVAN
dialogue.line: HAS HE GONE MAD?! DAMN! I HAVE TO GET HIM OUT!

dialogue.speaker: EVAN
dialogue.line: WHAT KIND OF BULLSHIT IS THAT?! HOW IS GETTING EATEN THE ANSWER?

dialogue.speaker: HEADON
dialogue.line: HE MUST FACE THE EEL WITHOUT STRUGGLING! THAT IS THE ONLY WAY TO PASS THIS TEST! BUT IN ORDER TO DO THAT, HE NEEDS ENORMOUS COURAGE, DETERMINATION, AND JUDGMENT! THAT'S WHAT THIS TEST IS ABOUT!

dialogue.speaker: EVAN
dialogue.line: RIGHT, MR. HEADON? THAT'S A GENEROUS ASSESSMENT.

dialogue.speaker: HEADON
dialogue.line: EVAN, YOU, WHY DIDN'T YOU TELL HIM ABOUT THE PATH FROM THE START?

dialogue.speaker: HEADON
dialogue.line: IT DOESN'T REALLY HELP. USUALLY, MOST OF THE TIME, EVEN IF THEY CAN THINK OF THE PATH, THEY CAN'T FOLLOW THROUGH WITH IT. COURAGE AND DETERMINATION AREN'T SO EASY TO COME BY. COURAGE AND BRAVERY COME FROM EXHAUSTIVE TRAINING. BUT WHAT HE HAS IS NOTHING LIKE COURAGE OR BRAVERY. THE FEAR OF LOSING THE ONE HE'S LOOKING FOR IS SWALLOWING HIS OTHER FEARS. HE'S OUT OF HIS MIND, THAT BOY.

dialogue.speaker: EVAN
dialogue.line: HEY! HEADON! WHAT'S WRONG WITH THAT BALL?! WHY WON'T IT POP?!

dialogue.speaker: EVAN
dialogue.line: "WHO KNOWS?" HEY, ARE YOU NUTS? A BALL THAT DOESN'T POP FROM THE BLACK MARCH?! EVEN I HAVEN'T SEEN ONE OF THOSE!

dialogue.speaker: HEADON
dialogue.line: WELL, MAYBE IT'S REALLY WELL MADE.

summary: Baam reflects on his past with Rachel before facing Headon's test. Observers Headon and Evan discuss the impossible nature of the test, which requires Baam to be swallowed by a giant eel and stab it from the inside with the Black March. Despite their doubts and attempts to make him give up, Baam surprisingly attempts this exact method, succeeding in stabbing the eel but failing to pop the ball, leaving the observers baffled.
